title: Config Show
description: When config discovery or path resolution behaves unexpectedly, users need to see exactly what specd resolved. The `specd config show` command prints the fully-resolved project configuration as loaded from `specd.yaml`, including absolute paths, workspace mappings, and approval gate settings.
context: When config discovery or path resolution behaves unexpectedly, users need to see exactly what specd resolved. The `specd config show` command prints the fully-resolved project configuration as loaded from `specd.yaml`, including absolute paths, workspace mappings, and approval gate settings.

rules:
```
specd config show [--format text|json|toon]
```

- `--format text|json|toon` — optional; output format, defaults to `text`

In `text` mode (default), the command prints the resolved config as structured key-value output:

```
projectRoot:  <absolute-path>
specdPath:    <absolute-path>
schemaRef:    <schema-ref>
approvals:    spec=<true|false>  signoff=<true|false>

workspaces:
  <name>  <ownership>  [type: <adapter-type>]  config: <serialized-config>
  ...

storage:
  changes:   [type: <adapter-type>]  config: <serialized-config>
  drafts:    [type: <adapter-type>]  config: <serialized-config>
  discarded: [type: <adapter-type>]  config: <serialized-config>
  archive:   [type: <adapter-type>]  config: <serialized-config>
```

Optional fields are shown only when present in the config:

```
storage:
  pattern: <archive-pattern>

context:
  file: <path>
  instruction: <text>
  ...

contextIncludeSpecs: <pattern>, <pattern>, ...
contextExcludeSpecs: <pattern>, <pattern>, ...
llmOptimizedContext: <true|false>
schemaPlugins: <ref>, <ref>, ...

plugins:
  agents:
    <name>  (present)
    ...

schemaOverrides: (present)
```

The text view is intentionally summarized. It shows the resolved workspace and storage paths used by humans for diagnosis, but it does not expand the full adapter-binding structure.

In `json` or `toon` mode, the command MUST serialize the `SpecdConfig` object directly — no manual field selection. The output is the config as-is, encoded in the respective format. This ensures that new fields added to `SpecdConfig`, including named adapter bindings such as `specsAdapter`, `schemasAdapter`, `changesAdapter`, `draftsAdapter`, `discardedAdapter`, and `archiveAdapter`, appear automatically without CLI changes. Optional fields that are `undefined` are omitted by the serializer.
The config contains no sensitive values — all fields are filesystem paths, schema references, and boolean flags. No redaction is required.

If the config cannot be loaded (discovery failure or parse error), exits per the entrypoint exit code rules. This command is most useful precisely when config loading is suspected to be wrong — errors are printed with enough detail to diagnose the problem.

constraints: All paths in the output are absolute — the same values as in the resolved SpecdConfig

scenarios.name: No positional arguments
then: the command proceeds without positional arguments

scenarios.name: artifactRules not shown in text output
when: specd config show runs in text mode
then: the output does not include an artifactRules section

scenarios.name: plugins shown in text output
given: a config with plugins.agents containing one entry
when: specd config show runs in text mode
then: the output includes a plugins section listing the agent names

scenarios.name: No sensitive values in config output
then: the output contains only filesystem paths, schema references, and boolean flags

scenarios.name: Config not found
given: CWD is not under any directory containing specd.yaml
then: AND stderr contains an error: message describing the discovery failure
